query: How do I make a straw geometric connector sculpture by cutting straws and threading string through them to create 3D shapes?
task: Straw geometric connector sculpture – cut straws and thread string through to make 3D shapes
goal: Build a stable three-dimensional geometric sculpture from evenly cut straw segments joined with string.

steps.text: Gather the materials and cut the straws into the needed consistent lengths.
completion_cues: Usable straw segments and string are ready.
incompletion_cues: Materials are missing or segment lengths are not prepared.

steps.text: Thread and connect straw segments into the initial flat geometric units.
completion_cues: String passes through connected segments forming base polygons.
incompletion_cues: Segments remain loose or the base geometry is open.

steps.text: Join the geometric units into a three-dimensional structure.
completion_cues: Connected faces hold a recognizable 3D form.
incompletion_cues: Only flat or disconnected units are present.

steps.text: Tighten and tie the string, trim excess, and check the sculpture's stability.
completion_cues: Knots are secure and the structure holds its shape.
incompletion_cues: Connections are loose, untied, or unstable.

completion_evidence: Straws, scissors, and other craft supplies are visible together on the worktable.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No prepared straw segments are visible yet.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Clear a small work area and begin cutting the straws to consistent lengths.

completion_evidence: Two long straws, scissors, thread, and a small card are arranged in the work area.
After using the phone, the person has returned their hands to the craft materials.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The visible straws remain full length and no prepared set of segments is visible.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Cut the long straws into consistent segments before beginning to thread them.

completion_evidence: Several blue and green straw segments have been cut and arranged by length.
The person repeatedly compared and trimmed the segments.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No string has been threaded through the segments.
The person has left the craft table and is handling a snack at the end of the observed interval.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Return to the sorted segments, finish any needed length corrections, and start threading the first polygon.

completion_evidence: The person has returned from the kitchen to the craft table.
Multiple short blue and green straw segments are spread out and being organized by hand.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No threaded polygon or connected straw unit is visible.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Use the prepared segments and string to thread and close the first flat geometric unit.

query: How do I make a tissue paper flower?
goal: Shape stacked tissue paper into a secured and evenly fluffed decorative flower.

steps.text: Stack and align the tissue paper layers.
completion_cues: Multiple tissue sheets form an aligned stack.
incompletion_cues: Sheets are separate or substantially misaligned.

steps.text: Accordion-fold the stacked tissue paper into an even strip.
completion_cues: The full stack is folded into repeated pleats.
incompletion_cues: A substantial portion remains unfolded or pleats are coming apart.

steps.text: Secure the center and prepare the folded ends for shaping.
completion_cues: The pleated strip is firmly bound at its center and ready to open.
incompletion_cues: The center is unsecured or the stack cannot retain its folds.

steps.text: Separate and fluff the layers into a balanced flower shape.
completion_cues: Tissue layers are opened into visible petals around the center.
incompletion_cues: Layers remain compressed or the flower is only partly opened.

completion_evidence: Purple and light-blue tissue sheets are on the table and are being layered and aligned.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The person is still repositioning separate sheets at the end of the interval.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Finish aligning the full stack, then begin even accordion folds along one edge.

completion_evidence: The alternating purple and light-blue sheets have been consolidated into one aligned stack.
A narrow pleat has been formed along the near edge.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: Most of the sheet stack remains flat and unfolded.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Continue alternating the folds across the full stack with an even pleat width.

completion_evidence: The full tissue stack has been folded into a long, narrow accordion strip.
The person is pressing the final pleats flat and keeping their edges aligned.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The center has not yet been visibly tied or otherwise secured.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Bind the middle of the finished accordion strip firmly before opening the layers.

completion_evidence: The accordion strip is tied tightly at its center.
Purple and light-blue layers have been fanned out and many are separated into rounded petal sections.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The person is still separating and redistributing layers, and the two sides are not yet evenly fluffed.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Gently separate the remaining compressed layers and balance the petals around the secured center.

query: How do I make a ham and cheese croissant sandwich?
task: Making a ham and cheese croissant sandwich
goal: Assemble a croissant sandwich with ham and cheese enclosed between the split halves.

steps.text: Gather the croissant, ham, cheese, plate, and cutting utensil.
completion_cues: The sandwich ingredients and needed utensil are accessible.
incompletion_cues: A required ingredient or utensil is unavailable.

steps.text: Split the croissant into connected or separate top and bottom halves.
completion_cues: The croissant has an opening suitable for filling.
incompletion_cues: The croissant remains closed or is being cut incorrectly.

steps.text: Layer ham and cheese on the bottom half.
completion_cues: Both ham and cheese are positioned inside the croissant.
incompletion_cues: Either filling is still absent or outside the croissant.

steps.text: Close the croissant and place the assembled sandwich for serving.
completion_cues: The top half covers the fillings and the sandwich is assembled.
incompletion_cues: The sandwich remains open or fillings need repositioning.

completion_evidence: A croissant has been removed from its package and placed on a white plate.
Ham and cheese containers are visible beside the plate.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No cutting utensil has been placed with the ingredients yet.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Retrieve a suitable knife and position the croissant for a controlled horizontal cut.

completion_evidence: A table knife has been retrieved from the drawer.
The knife is being worked horizontally through the side of the croissant while the other hand stabilizes it.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The croissant has not yet been opened into clear top and bottom halves.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Finish the horizontal cut without severing or crushing the halves, then open the croissant for filling.

completion_evidence: The croissant is fully split and both cut faces are open on the plate.
After adjusting the television, the person returned to the counter, opened the ham container, and began separating a ham slice.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No ham or cheese is yet visibly placed on the croissant.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Place the ham and cheese on the bottom half, then close the top half over them.

completion_evidence: Ham and a square cheese slice were placed across the bottom croissant half.
The top half has been set over the fillings and pressed into alignment on the plate.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: none

query: How do I make a classic peanut butter and honey sandwich?

steps.text: Gather two bread slices, peanut butter, honey, a plate, and a spreading utensil.
completion_cues: Bread, both fillings, plate, and utensil are ready.
incompletion_cues: A required ingredient or utensil is missing.

steps.text: Spread peanut butter over one bread slice.
completion_cues: One slice has a usable peanut butter layer.
incompletion_cues: Peanut butter has not been applied or remains unspread.

steps.text: Add honey over the sandwich filling area.
completion_cues: Honey is visibly applied with the peanut butter.
incompletion_cues: Honey has not yet been added or is outside the filling area.

steps.text: Close, align, and lightly press the bread slices for serving.
completion_cues: The fillings are enclosed by aligned bread slices.
incompletion_cues: The slices remain separate or need alignment.

incompletion_or_error_evidence: Two bread slices have not yet been placed on the plate.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Remove two slices onto the plate and place the peanut butter, honey, and spreading utensil within reach.

completion_evidence: The bread bag has been put aside, leaving two slices on the plate.
Peanut butter and honey containers are now upright beside the plate, and the person is reaching for a utensil.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: Neither filling has been applied to the bread.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Open the peanut butter and spread an even layer across one bread slice.

completion_evidence: Peanut butter was squeezed onto one slice and spread across its surface with a knife.

The second slice was placed on top, aligned, and pressed repeatedly; the person then began putting utensils away.

query: How do I make tahini oat snack balls?
goal: Combine tahini and oats into a cohesive mixture and portion it into snack balls.

steps.text: Gather and measure the snack-ball ingredients and mixing tools.
completion_cues: The ingredients and a suitable bowl and utensil are ready.
incompletion_cues: Ingredients or mixing tools are missing or unmeasured.

steps.text: Combine and mix the ingredients into a cohesive mixture.
completion_cues: The ingredients form a sufficiently uniform, shapeable mixture.
incompletion_cues: Ingredients remain separate, dry, or unevenly mixed.

steps.text: Portion the mixture and roll it into individual balls.
completion_cues: The mixture is divided into rounded portions.
incompletion_cues: Much of the mixture remains unportioned or portions are not shaped.

steps.text: Arrange the finished snack balls and complete any required setting or storage.
completion_cues: Finished balls are arranged and ready to eat or set.
incompletion_cues: Shaped portions still require arranging or setting.

completion_evidence: A mixing bowl, measuring cup, oats, tahini-like liquid ingredients, and utensils are arranged on the counter.
The person is pouring oats into the measuring cup.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The measured oats have not yet been transferred to the mixing bowl.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Finish measuring the oats into the bowl, then measure the remaining ingredients.

completion_evidence: A measured cup of oats has been emptied into the mixing bowl.
A pale, thick liquid has been measured in the cup and poured over the oats.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: Other staged liquid ingredients remain outside the bowl, and mixing has not started.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Measure and add the remaining ingredients, then begin combining them in the bowl.

completion_evidence: Oats and the measured pale liquid are in the mixing bowl.
An amber sweetener has been squeezed onto the dry surface in a visible spiral.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The current dialog identifies the added sweetener as honey rather than the intended maple syrup.
The ingredients remain unmixed.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Before mixing, remove as much of the unintended sweetener as practical and add the intended measured sweetener.

completion_evidence: Maple syrup was subsequently measured and added before the ingredients were stirred into a combined mixture.
Several rounded oat portions have been compacted by hand and placed on a separate plate.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The mixture is visibly crumbly during rolling.
Unportioned mixture remains in the bowl while another ball is still being formed.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Compress the remaining portions firmly, finish rolling them, and arrange all completed balls on the plate.

query: How do I adjust the tension of my window blinds?
goal: Adjust the blind so its controls operate smoothly and the blind holds a balanced position.

steps.text: Inspect the blind and establish how its lift and tilt currently respond.
completion_cues: The current movement and resistance have been checked.
incompletion_cues: The blind response has not been tested.

steps.text: Make a small tension adjustment using the blind's applicable control.
completion_cues: A deliberate incremental tension change has been made.
incompletion_cues: No adjustment has been made or the control is being forced.

steps.text: Cycle the blind through its range and assess movement and holding behavior.
completion_cues: The blind has been raised, lowered, and checked after adjustment.
incompletion_cues: Post-adjustment movement has not been fully tested.

steps.text: Fine-tune if needed and confirm smooth, balanced operation.
completion_cues: The blind moves smoothly and holds without excessive resistance or drift.
incompletion_cues: Movement remains stiff, uneven, or unable to hold position.

completion_evidence: The blind begins partly raised and is pulled down until it covers nearly the full window.
The person handles the lower rail and control near the bottom while observing the slats.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: A full lift-and-tilt response check has not yet been demonstrated.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Test the tilt response and raise the blind partway to establish its current resistance and holding behavior.

completion_evidence: Twisting the wand visibly changes the slats from closed to open and back toward closed.
The blind has also been raised partway and lowered while the person watches the rail response.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No complete post-adjustment up-and-down cycle has been observed.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Make only a small further control adjustment, then cycle the blind through a broader range to check resistance.

completion_evidence: The bottom rail has been moved from near the sill to approximately mid-window.
The wand is then twisted repeatedly while the slats move toward a closed angle.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The current dialog directs continued twisting until the wand becomes stiff and unresponsive, indicating an excessive adjustment rather than a controlled small change.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Stop forcing the wand, reverse it slightly to relieve resistance, and retest the slat response gently.

completion_evidence: The wand direction is reversed and the slats visibly reopen from the tightly closed position.
The bottom rail is subsequently lifted from mid-window toward the headrail.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The requested full raise-and-lower test is incomplete; the observed interval ends during the upward movement.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Finish raising the blind, lower it fully without forcing the controls, and confirm smooth balanced travel.

query: How do I install a key hook by the door?
task: Installing a key hook by the door
goal: Mount a level, secure key hook at a practical location beside the door.

steps.text: Choose the hook location, level the fixture, and mark its mounting point or points.
completion_cues: A suitable level location has clear mounting marks.
incompletion_cues: The location is undecided, unlevel, or unmarked.

steps.text: Prepare the marked mounting point with a pilot hole or wall anchor as needed.
completion_cues: The wall is prepared to accept the fastener.
incompletion_cues: The marked point is not prepared or the anchor is insecure.

steps.text: Position the hook and drive its fastener until the fixture is secure.
completion_cues: The hook is attached firmly to the wall.
incompletion_cues: The fixture is detached, loose, or only partly fastened.

steps.text: Check alignment and stability, then test the hook with keys.
completion_cues: The mounted hook stays level and supports keys without movement.
incompletion_cues: The hook needs tightening, leveling, or a load test.

completion_evidence: The decorative hook plaque has been picked up and the wall area beside the doorway is being inspected.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The plaque is not held against the chosen position and no mounting mark is visible.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Hold the plaque at a practical height, level it, and mark the mounting point.

completion_evidence: The plaque is held against the selected wall area and a red pencil mark is made along its upper mounting position.
The plaque and pencil are set back down after marking.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No level check is visible before the plaque is removed from the wall.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Verify the marked position is level, correcting the mark if needed before drilling or anchoring.

completion_evidence: A phone level is held horizontally across the marked area.
Its display turns green and shows 0 degrees before the phone is set down.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The wall has not yet been prepared for a fastener.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Set the phone aside and prepare the verified mark with the appropriate hole or anchor.

completion_evidence: After using the phone for another activity, the person returns to the level and again obtains a green 0-degree reading at the marks.
A small wall fastener or anchor is placed at the mark and tapped repeatedly with a hammer.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The decorative hook plaque is still off the wall and the fastener preparation has not been visibly load-checked.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Confirm the fastener is seated correctly, position the plaque over it, and secure the fixture firmly.

query: How do I put a harness on a dog?
task: How to put a harness on a dog
goal: Place and adjust a correctly oriented harness on the dog with a secure, comfortable fit.

steps.text: Identify the harness orientation, open the fasteners, and loosen the straps as needed.
completion_cues: The harness is oriented and open enough to put on without force.
incompletion_cues: The harness is twisted, closed, or too tight to place safely.

steps.text: Guide the harness onto the dog and position it around the neck, chest, and legs as designed.
completion_cues: The harness lies in the intended position on the dog's body.
incompletion_cues: The harness is off the dog, misplaced, or being forced.

steps.text: Connect all required buckles or clips.
completion_cues: All designed closures are fully engaged.
incompletion_cues: A required buckle remains open or incorrectly connected.

steps.text: Adjust the straps and check for a snug, untwisted, comfortable fit.
completion_cues: The harness is secure, untwisted, and has appropriate clearance.
incompletion_cues: The fit is loose, restrictive, twisted, or unchecked.

completion_evidence: A stuffed dog is placed upright on the table and a red harness is picked up and spread between both hands.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The harness is still being examined and has not been opened or placed on the dog.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Identify the neck and body openings, release the closures, and loosen the straps before placement.

completion_evidence: After an unsuccessful tight placement attempt, the harness is removed from the stuffed dog.
The person then works the red strap through its adjuster to enlarge the opening.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The harness is still off the dog and strap loosening is ongoing.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Finish loosening the opening, confirm the straps are untwisted, and guide the harness on without force.

completion_evidence: The harness has been substantially loosened and oriented flat before a new placement attempt.
It is now positioned around the stuffed dog's neck and chest while the person manipulates the side closures.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The hands remain on the buckle area and a fully connected, untwisted fit has not yet been demonstrated.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Finish engaging each required closure, then smooth and adjust the straps before checking clearance.

completion_evidence: The person inspects the harness position around the neck and chest, then removes it to work the strap through an adjuster.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: At the end of the interval the adjusted harness is entirely off the dog, so closure and fit cannot be confirmed.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Complete the off-dog strap adjustment, replace the harness in the correct orientation, and fasten all closures before checking fit.

query: How do I put on cufflinks?
goal: Fasten cufflinks through both aligned shirt cuffs so each link is secure and correctly oriented.

steps.text: Open each cuff and align its cufflink holes.
completion_cues: The corresponding holes are accessible and aligned.
incompletion_cues: The cuff remains buttoned, folded incorrectly, or misaligned.

steps.text: Orient the first cufflink and prepare its post or toggle for insertion.
completion_cues: The first link is oriented with its fastener ready to pass through.
incompletion_cues: The link orientation or fastener position prevents insertion.

steps.text: Insert and lock the cufflink through the first cuff.
completion_cues: The link passes through both sides and its fastener is locked.
incompletion_cues: The post has not cleared both holes or remains unlocked.

steps.text: Repeat on the other cuff and check both cufflinks for security and alignment.
completion_cues: Both cufflinks are installed, aligned, and secure.
incompletion_cues: The second cuff is unfinished or either link is loose.

completion_evidence: The white shirt has been laid flat and smoothed across the work surface.
One cuff is opened and lifted for access.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The cufflink holes have not yet been visibly aligned together.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Open the cuff fully and align its corresponding holes before handling the cufflink.

completion_evidence: A cufflink is picked up and brought to the cuff.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The cuff remains folded over itself while insertion is attempted, leaving the corresponding holes difficult to align.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Remove the cufflink, unfold the cuff completely, and align both holes before reorienting the post for insertion.

completion_evidence: The cufflink is withdrawn from the folded attempt and the cuff is opened and laid flatter.
The post is then worked through the aligned cuff holes while the fastener is manipulated on the back side.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The hands remain on the first cufflink and a locked fastener has not been clearly demonstrated.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Rotate the first fastener fully into its locked position, tug-test it, and then prepare the other cuff.

completion_evidence: The first gold cufflink is visibly seated through its cuff and the cuff is laid flat after handling.
The second cuff is opened, its holes are brought together, and the second cufflink post is worked through them.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The person is still manipulating the second link at the end of the interval, with no final lock or security check visible.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Lock the second cufflink toggle, then gently tug and align both installed links.

query: How do I use commas correctly in a list?
task: Use commas correctly in a list
goal: Write and review a clear multi-item list with correctly placed commas and a consistent serial-comma choice.

steps.text: Write a sentence containing a series of three or more distinct items.
completion_cues: A complete multi-item series is present for punctuation.
incompletion_cues: The series is absent or has fewer than three items.

steps.text: Place commas between the listed items.
completion_cues: Each internal boundary between list items is punctuated.
incompletion_cues: Item boundaries are missing commas or commas split individual items.

steps.text: Handle the final conjunction and serial comma consistently.
completion_cues: The punctuation before the final conjunction follows a consistent intended style.
incompletion_cues: The last item boundary is ambiguous or inconsistent.

steps.text: Review the sentence for list clarity and punctuation errors.
completion_cues: The full series has been checked and reads unambiguously.
incompletion_cues: The written list has not been reviewed or still has visible punctuation issues.

completion_evidence: A notebook is opened to a blank page, a pen is picked up, and writing begins near the top of the page.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No complete multi-item series is yet visible.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Write a complete sentence containing at least three distinct list items.

completion_evidence: A four-item series has been written across the first notebook line.
Visible comma marks separate the internal items and a comma appears before the final conjunction.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No separate review pass has yet been observed.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Read the complete line back and verify every item boundary and the final conjunction punctuation.

completion_evidence: The four-item line remains complete in the notebook and the pen has been put down.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The person switches to a phone weather screen instead of reviewing the written punctuation.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: Set the phone aside and review the written list item by item for missing or misplaced commas.

completion_evidence: After the weather and laptop diversions, the person returns to the notebook and points the pen along the existing item boundaries.
The written punctuation is revisited, additional notes or corrections are completed, and the pen is finally put down.
sampled_chunk_states.recovery_action: none
